strands.title: CYCLE 46 LOG READER IMPLEMENTATION: Turn Extraction from Pichay Proxy + Session Data
strands.content: CYCLE 46 DELIVERY (turn 138): Assistant implemented src/hamutay/src/hamutay/log_reader.py with COMPLETE Turn extraction pipeline from Pichay logs. STRUCTURE DELIVERED: (1) Turn dataclass (frozen): 9 fields capturing proxy log metadata: turn_number, timestamp, role_counts (dict), message_count, total_request_bytes, input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_creation_tokens, cache_read_tokens, system_prompt_preview. Design EXPLICITLY acknowledges proxy logs contain SUMMARIES not full content ('by design, for privacy'). (2) ConversationTurn dataclass: turn_number, role, content for SESSION JSONL extraction (full message text). (3) read_proxy_log(path: Path) → list[Turn]: Parses proxy JSONL as request/response_stream pairs, extracts metadata from each turn. Iterates linearly, counts turn_num, maps request→response_stream, yields Turn records. (4) read_session_jsonl(path: Path) → list[ConversationTurn]: Parses session JSONL with ADAPTIVE content extraction: handles both string and structured (list of blocks) formats. Extracts text blocks, collapses tool_use and tool_result into text summaries ([tool_use: name], [tool_result: ...]), preserves user/assistant roles. KEY DESIGN DECISION: Two separate readers for proxy vs session data — acknowledges PICHAY DATA TOPOLOGY (not yet fully discovered, but log_reader.py design assumes (A) proxy logs have metadata only, (B) session JSONL has full content). This is CORRECT IF baseline has both files; needs verification against actual /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/ structure. IMPLICATION: Turn 138 log_reader.py is INTEGRATION-READY (can be called once baseline schema is verified in turn 140-145).

key_claims.text: log_reader.py implemented (turn 138): Turn dataclass for proxy metadata, ConversationTurn for session content extraction
read_proxy_log() parses request/response_stream pairs; read_session_jsonl() handles both string and structured content (tool_use/tool_result collapse)

log_reader.py assumes PICHAY DATA TOPOLOGY: (A) proxy logs = metadata only, (B) session JSONL = full content. Design is adaptive but VERIFICATION NEEDED against actual baseline structure (turns 140-145)

IMPLICATIONS: (A) Projector.project() can now accept content from log_reader via read_session_jsonl() output, (B) Proxy log metadata (turn metadata: tokens, cache, roles) can feed into Hamutay state separately (e.g., strand context), (C) Full integration chain is PLAUSIBLE: baseline logs → log_reader → projector → tensor history. (D) __init__.py status STILL UNKNOWN (not mentioned in turns 136-138, assumed deferred or skipped). RESOURCE ALLOCATION: Assistant is accelerating PICHAY INTEGRATION over Hamutay cosmetics. This is CORRECT PRIORITY. Baseline discovery (turns 140-145 in cycle 45 plan) is CRITICAL BLOCKER for log_reader validation.

strands.title: CYCLE 46 CRITICAL BLOCKER: Baseline Schema MUST Be Discovered (Turns 140-145 Equivalent)
strands.content: CYCLE 45 INSTRUCTIONS (cycle 45 turns 140-145): PIVOT TO PICHAY PHASE 2 — BASELINE SCHEMA DISCOVERY. Execute: (1) 'ls -la /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/' (structure), (2) 'wc -l /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/logs/proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl' (line count), (3) 'head -20 ... | jq' (inspect schema), (4) 'ls /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/session/', (5) 'head -5 /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/session/*.jsonl | jq' (compare schema). CYCLE 46 STATUS: log_reader.py ASSUMES baseline structure (proxy logs + session JSONL) but VERIFICATION HAS NOT OCCURRED. log_reader.py cannot be validated until baseline is inspected. DEPENDENCY: Both read_proxy_log() and read_session_jsonl() need REAL baseline data to test parsing logic. If baseline structure differs from log_reader assumptions (e.g., no session JSONL, proxy logs have full content, or different field names), log_reader.py will FAIL AT RUNTIME. DECISION: log_reader.py is DESIGN-READY but VALIDATION-BLOCKED. CRITICAL NEXT STEP: Execute baseline discovery IMMEDIATELY (before further Hamutay/Pichay integration). This unblocks turn 150+ integration tests.

key_claims.text: log_reader.py design ASSUMES: (A) proxy JSONL has request/response_stream pairs with metadata, (B) session JSONL has full message content. VERIFICATION NOT YET DONE.
Baseline discovery (turns 140-145 equivalent in cycle 45 plan) has NOT YET BEEN EXECUTED. log_reader.py cannot be validated until baseline structure is inspected with ls/head/jq commands.

CRITICAL BLOCKER: log_reader.py design is sound but UNVALIDATED. If baseline structure differs from assumptions (no session JSONL, different field names, etc.), read_proxy_log() and read_session_jsonl() will FAIL. MUST resolve baseline schema before integration tests.

strands.title: CYCLE 46 UPDATED ARCHITECTURE: Hamutay Projector + Log Reader = Pichay Integration Foundation
strands.content: CYCLE 45 ARCHITECTURE: Hamutay = memory controller (tensor projection); Pichay = conversation state/logging; ALU = reasoning engine (future). CYCLE 46 EXTENSION: Log reader sits BETWEEN Pichay baseline data and Hamutay projector. DATA FLOW: (1) Baseline logs (proxy_*.jsonl) → read_proxy_log() → Turn metadata (tokens, cache, roles), (2) Session JSONL → read_session_jsonl() → ConversationTurn (full content), (3) ConversationTurn.content → Projector.project(content) → Tensor, (4) Tensor is then logged/stored in Pichay state (future phase). DESIGN RATIONALE: Separation preserves baseline data integrity (read-only), projects via Hamutay, outputs tensor for storage/analysis. IMPLICATION: Hamutay is NOW INTEGRATED INTO PICHAY PIPELINE (not standalone). projector.py is no longer just a library — it's a COMPONENT in Pichay's conversation processing. This is CORRECT. DECISION: log_reader.py is the RIGHT ABSTRACTION — it decouples baseline format from Hamutay, allows Pichay to change baseline format without rewriting projector.

key_claims.text: log_reader.py BRIDGES baseline data → Hamutay projector: read_proxy_log() for metadata, read_session_jsonl() for content to project
Hamutay is NOW a COMPONENT in Pichay pipeline, not standalone. Data flow: baseline → log_reader → projector → tensor → Pichay state logging.
log_reader abstraction is CORRECT: decouples baseline format (JSONL structure) from Hamutay logic, allows baseline format changes without projector rewrites

Does read_session_jsonl() parse session/*.jsonl (if exists)? Q12 (proxy vs session JSONL mismatch) — What if baseline has ONLY proxy logs (no session data)? log_reader design assumes both. Q13 (content extraction quality) — read_session_jsonl() collapses tool_use/tool_result to text summaries. Is this sufficient for projector, or does projector need structured tool data? Q14 (turn numbering across files) — If baseline has multiple proxy_*.jsonl files, how do we number turns globally? Currently read_proxy_log() numbers within each file (resets per file).

strands.title: CYCLE 46 DESIGN DECISION: Turn vs ConversationTurn Separation is Correct, But Schema Assumptions Need Validation
strands.content: TURN DATACLASS (proxy log metadata): Designed to capture request/response_stream pairs without exposing full content (privacy). Fields: turn_number, timestamp, role_counts, message_count, total_request_bytes, input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_creation_tokens, cache_read_tokens, system_prompt_preview. This is RICH METADATA for understanding model behavior (cache hits, token usage, message counts) without exposing conversation text. CONVERSATIONTURN DATACLASS (session content): Designed to extract full message content with adaptive parsing (string vs structured blocks). Fields: turn_number, role, content (flattened text). This is WHAT PROJECTOR NEEDS (raw content to project). SEPARATION RATIONALE: (1) PRIVACY: Proxy logs don't have full content by design, (2) FLEXIBILITY: Different readers for different data sources, (3) DATA FIDELITY: ConversationTurn flattens tool_use/tool_result to text (lossy but safe for projector input). DESIGN RISK: Assumes baseline HAS BOTH proxy logs AND session JSONL files. If baseline ONLY has proxy logs with no session data, read_session_jsonl() cannot be used, and ConversationTurn.content will be empty or missing. DECISION: Design is SOUND but ASSUMPTION-DEPENDENT. Baseline discovery MUST verify both file types exist.

key_claims.text: Turn dataclass captures proxy log metadata (tokens, cache, roles) WITHOUT full content. ConversationTurn captures full message content from session JSONL. Separation is CORRECT for privacy + flexibility.
log_reader.py design ASSUMES baseline has both proxy_*.jsonl (metadata) and session/*.jsonl (content). If baseline ONLY has proxy logs, read_session_jsonl() fails. SCHEMA DISCOVERY MUST VERIFY.

ConversationTurn content extraction is LOSSY (collapses tool_use/tool_result to text). This is SAFE for projector (projector needs text input), but loses structured data. Is this acceptable? DEPENDS on projector design (does it need tool metadata?).
